item.title: OH NO THEY'RE CUTE
item.content:encoded:
Subsequent imaging of the SCP-2822-B population found that three "mature" 2822-B individuals were carrying small stones within their hollow cavity.

ogodhhg ;;;_;;;
Tell them I love them.
+1

Gee, never thought I'd get this far. I just want to thank everyone who read my article!

I'd love to tell you that I've got more writing in mind, but unfortunately I'm a first-year university student now, so I'm hopelessly busy.

Hope you are all enjoying life!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very cute! Hearty +1 :')

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is adorable, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like Coryn deserves way more credit. I loved the idea of rock sheep and rock sheep babies and that was 100% Coryn's idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, she did have a big part to play in helping get my idea worked out the way I wanted it to. She knows how to get the feels across better than me…

But I don't have her level of experience, so that's to be expected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This comes across as very genuine and emotional. I am not the least bit surprised that Zyn had a hand in writing this. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/thumbsup for atomicthumbs ;)

Glad you liked the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Zyn and I did our best to make it good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is just nice. I like a well-done nice SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: baby sheeeeep rooooccckkkssss~ <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bits about the woman and her gravestone aren't the best part of the article, but pan out by the end.

Yeah, we made some edits based on chat reviews to tone down the initial sappiness. I did really like Coryn's idea for the kindly old lady who gives the rock sheep baths though, so I wanted to keep that. The gravestone part was a nod towards the kind of level of self-understanding the rock shepherds have. They earn the sapient tag, but the Foundation didn't find that out until several months after discovery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, thanks! Most of the improvement was Zyn's doing, but I'm glad to get your seal of approval.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I saw this in chat, it left me cold, but you've really improved it. The bits about the woman and her gravestone aren't the best part of the article, but pan out by the end. It's focusing on the connection between A and B that makes this, though, and it's cute and sweet and not easily summed up as any one thing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really needed something like this right now. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry to hear you didn't think reading the article was worth your time or emotional expense. I hope you can find something you will enjoy in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guess you could say you have a heart of stone. Bah-dum-tsh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. You could have just said "Herding, except with rocks, and something mildly sappy" and saved the reader 984 words for all the emotional impact this had on me. Maybe I'm just tired and not in the mood for something sweet-ish like this, but it did nothing for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it so much. This is so sweet and good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always love conservationist!Foundation articles. Shudder to think of the GOC finding these guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strange and Beautiful. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such a simple idea but it was interesting…and kind of cute. I really enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Daww, little rock babies… that's worth an upvote in itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmmmm

Rock Shepards is an idea that is simple enough that you'd have thought it would have been done before. Regardless, this is a cute article, with a neat anomaly and a decent enough backstory. I'd love to see pictures or artwork of 2822-A and 2822-B instances, as the imagery conjured by the article is quite lovely.

+1. Solid article heh… solid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image is from Wikipedia, and is public domain. Image information here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MontBlancdeCheilon01.jpg

So, I basically am here because I thought rock husbandry was a really cool idea and this sort of pastoral (PUN INTENDEDDDD) article was something I'd been wanting to write for awhile. I think I was a couple lines into critting Coryn's draft in chat when I decided "you know what, I really like this" and offered the co-authoring. Collabs are fun, everyone!

Coryn had a ton of ideas I really liked, turns out. We did do some trimming—at some point we had rocksheepdogs, and hybrid vigor, and so on… anyone interested in doing co-authorship in the future, here's the collaborative sandbox we used to keep our stuff together: http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/coryn02-zyn-collaboration

Of course, big thanks to all of our reviewers, especially everyone who cooed over the flocks of rocks (credit WJS for that phrase, I loves it) in chat. You guys are the best! ^w^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, everyone! Cory here, and thanks for reading my SCP! I'd like to thank Zyn for all the help she gave me in making this article, as without her guidance it would likely not have seen the light of day. Also, you can thank EldritchCadence, ghostpage, SpectralDragon, Jekeled, Cimmerian, WrongJohnSilver, shayshay, Docmaster16, MegalomaniacInchworm, DrJets, Green_Smith, and everyone else for all their opinions and corrections in chat while it was being written. You're the best!

The image was taken from Wikipedia and is of Mont Blanc de Cheilon, a mountain in the Alps. The photo falls under Creative Commons (and is thus safe to use) and was found by Cimmerian. So there you go, something else to thank them for.

This is probably something this website gets a lot, but this is my first article. I was understandably quite nervous writing, since I wasn't sure my idea was even good enough! My original idea still had the basics it did now, but was meant to be more troubling and didn't have a real story to it. I did have quite a bit of fun writing; as formal as I tend to be in real life, writing in the very scientific tone an SCP article demands was a unique challenge.

After asking around, I got early approval on my idea and began writing on my own. Later, when asking for criticism, Zyn met me and proposed a collaboration. Working with her helped me learn a lot about what writing a good SCP is about, and helped me get around a misconception I held about them: SCPs aren't always terrifying, or deadly, or even strange: but they do fall outside the purview of our world, even if they can be found endearing (though a lot of them are a combination of those first three). Finding the lack of positivity in the SCP articles makes me happy to know I could write a bit of fresh air into an article. As much as the articles that haunt your sleepless nights are what I love to read, knowing the Foundation isn't all bad makes some of my favorite articles.

I'm completely open to questions and constructive criticism, so ask away! I may also write supplementary material for the article at some point, if you're interested in that. For now, let's just be happy I finally have the article posted. Thanks again!
